description:
This diagram illustrates the temporal update strategy for L2a data within the European Ground Motion Service (EGMS), showing the progression of time series data and associated processing windows. The horizontal axis represents time, with key years labelled: 2015, 2020, 2021, and 2022. The vertical axis represents data values of a continuous time series, depicted by green triangles, which show an initial decreasing trend, followed by a relatively stable period, and then a slight increase towards the end.

Three main temporal phases are indicated by horizontal arrows below the time series:
- The "Baseline" period is defined from 2015 to 2020 (light green arrow).
- The "1st Update" period spans from 2015 to 2021 (dark blue arrow).
- The "2nd Update" period spans from 2018 to 2022 (yellow arrow).

Above the time series, three overlapping rectangular boxes represent data processing windows for these updates:
- A light blue box covers the earliest data from before 2015 up to approximately 2018.
- An olive green box covers data from approximately 2017 to 2020, overlapping with the light blue box and the "Baseline" period.
- A light yellow box covers data from approximately 2019 to 2021, overlapping with the olive green box and extending into the "1st Update" period.

This strategy involves freezing time series to baseline values, processing new images (e.g., from 2021) with recent baseline data, stitching new time series for smoother conjunction, and re-evaluating derived metrics such as velocity, acceleration, and seasonality across the updated entire time series.
